title: Constituent Assembly
lead: The Constituent Assembly was not simply a drafting body — it was a ==nation-building forum== that converted the freedom struggle into an enforceable constitutional architecture.

items.label: members responsible for drafting India's Constitution
first sitting, under the Cabinet Mission Plan
of meetings — 101 days discussing the constitutional text
amendments dealt with, as noted by Ambedkar
spent on consideration of the Draft Constitution
Constitution enacted; published as draft Feb 1948

head: Feature
Remarks
rows: Historical origin
Created under the ==Cabinet Mission Plan==, but became sovereign in spirit after Independence. First sat on ==9 December 1946==.
Representative but indirectly elected
Not elected through universal adult franchise — members from British Indian provinces were ==indirectly elected by provincial legislative assemblies==; princely states nominated representatives.
Committee-driven functioning
Rules of Procedure, Steering, Finance & Staff, Credentials, House, Order of Business Committees, and an Ad hoc Committee on the National Flag.
Drafting Committee & Ambedkar's role
The Drafting Committee scrutinised the draft prepared by Constitutional Adviser ==B. N. Rau==; ==Dr. B. R. Ambedkar==, as Chairman, spoke the most in the Assembly.
Stage-wise constitution-making
Preliminary stage, first, second and third readings; draft published Feb 1948, introduced Nov 1948, discussed ==clause by clause==, enacted 26 Nov 1949. (PRS)
==Objectives Resolution== as philosophical foundation
Introduced by ==Nehru on 13 December 1946==; laid down ideals of sovereignty, republicanism, democracy, justice and rights — later influenced the ==Preamble==.
Public participation
Constitution-making was not confined to the Assembly — archives record petitions and feedback from groups, organisations and individuals.
Dual role after Independence
After 15 August 1947, also functioned as the ==Dominion legislature== while continuing constitution-making.
Diverse but eminent membership

Lawyers, women, political leaders, social reformers, professors, former civil servants, religious minorities — Nehru, Ambedkar, Patel, K.M. Munshi, Sarojini Naidu, Hansa Mehta, Frank Anthony, Gurmukh Singh Musafir.
Encyclopaedic borrowing, indigenous vision

==UK== (parliamentary govt), ==US== (Fundamental Rights), ==Ireland== (DPSP), ==Canada== (residuary powers), ==Australia== (joint sitting), ==Germany== (emergency provisions), ==South Africa== (RS election).

head: Challenge
Nature of the problem
How the Assembly responded
rows: Partition & communal violence
Worked amid displacement, massacres and insecurity.
Emphasised ==secular citizenship==, minority safeguards and constitutional order.
Integration of princely states
British Indian provinces and princely states had to be unified in one framework.
Designed a Union capable of ==political integration== and administrative coherence.
Democracy for a poor, illiterate society
Many doubted universal democracy could work in India.
Adopted ==adult franchise== and parliamentary democracy.
Liberty vs social reform
Civil liberties needed alongside caste reform, land reform, affirmative action.
Combined ==Fundamental Rights with Directive Principles== and special provisions for disadvantaged groups.
Federalism vs national unity
Partition created fear of Balkanisation.
Adopted ==federalism with a strong Centre==.
Minority protection
Communal tensions made minority rights politically sensitive.
Religious freedom, cultural & educational rights, equality provisions.
Language question
Linguistic diversity made national-language debates contentious.
Compromise: ==Hindi, English and regional languages== each with constitutional roles.
Borrowing vs originality
Borrowing from global constitutions and colonial laws drew criticism of imitation.
Adapted borrowed institutions to Indian needs — a ==distinctive constitutional synthesis==.
Administrative continuity
Sudden rupture from colonial governance could create chaos.
Preserved continuity while shifting legitimacy ==from empire to people==.
Time pressure
Drafting amid crisis and transition.
Completed the Constitution in ==less than three years==.

blocks.text: The Constituent Assembly did not merely write India's Constitution; it wrote India's method of disagreement.

However good a Constitution may be, if those who are implementing it are not good, it will prove to be bad. However bad a Constitution may be, if those implementing it are good, it will prove to be good.
